insights.title: core.fm takes the input signal as the frequency modulator
insights.summary: The `core.fm` node uses its audio input signal as the modulation source. The `modulator` parameter is an additional gain applied to the incoming signal before FM is computed. The basic 2-operator pattern is: `oscillator -> ahdsr -> fm -> ahdsr`. For 3+ operators, chain additional fm nodes: `... -> fm -> ahdsr -> fm -> ahdsr`.
insights.verify_hint: Confirm that core.fm input signal = modulator source, and modulator parameter = gain multiplier.

insights.title: Implement FM feedback by routing signal back with a frame_block to minimise latency
insights.summary: To achieve FM feedback (carrier modulating itself), route the signal back to the input using a feedback path and wrap it in a `frame_block` to reduce the feedback latency to one sample. Without the frame_block the feedback delay equals the buffer size. This network should be compiled for acceptable CPU performance.
insights.verify_hint: Test feedback FM patch: confirm frame_block reduces feedback delay to 1 sample vs. buffer-size delay without it.

insights.title: core.fm is monophonic — use container.multi for stereo FM
insights.summary: Like most oscillator/synthesis nodes in scriptnode, `core.fm` processes a single channel. For stereo FM synthesis, place two `core.fm` nodes inside a `container.multi` which splits processing per channel.
insights.verify_hint: Confirm core.fm is single-channel by checking its channel configuration in the HISE source.
